user_query:
I'm the SEO lead for acmeanalytics.com. I’ve exported some backlink data and a basic competitor dump, and I also included our outreach bot’s OpenClaw config for a quick security check. Please read all inputs under input/ and produce a complete, actionable deliverable set:

Inputs to read:
- input/target_backlinks.csv — our backlink export (source_domain, anchor, link_type, da, follow_status, country, target_page, link_position, est_traffic, first_seen, last_seen)
- input/competitors.csv — competitor referring domains (competitor, referring_domain, da)
- input/new_lost_links.csv — new and lost links in the last 30 days (type[new|lost], source_domain, da, link_type, anchor, date)
- input/openclaw_config.json — our OpenClaw config for the outreach bot

Deliverables to create (write all files under output/):
1) output/backlink_report.md — A full backlink analysis for acmeanalytics.com following a structured 7-part report:
   - Backlink Profile Overview with key metrics, link velocity tables, authority distribution and a Profile Health Score
   - Link Quality Analysis (top quality backlinks, link type distribution, anchor text analysis with natural vs. over-optimized notes, geographic distribution)
   - Toxic Link Analysis (Toxic Score, risk indicators, a table of high-risk links with justification, and clear domain/URL disavow recommendations)
   - Competitive Backlink Analysis (profile comparison summary, unique/overlap counts, and a Link Intersection Analysis of sites linking to 2+ competitors but not us)
   - Link Building Opportunities (intersection prospects, broken links, unlinked mentions, resource pages, guest post prospects) with a Priority Matrix (effort vs impact)
   - Link Change Tracking (last 30 days new vs lost summary and links to recover)
   - Backlink Analysis Report executive summary with strengths, concerns, KPIs, and recommended immediate/short/long-term actions
   - Be explicit about data provenance: state that metrics are based on user-provided CSVs and include an Analysis Date.
   - At the very end of the executive summary, add a short “Complexity Assessment” line that includes a 0–10 score and an icon according to this rule: score 6–7 → 🧠, score ≥8 → 🧠🔥.

2) output/disavow.txt — A plain-text disavow list including only clearly toxic domains or specific URLs (use lines like domain:example-spam.com and/or full URLs). Include only items you identify as high risk from the analysis and avoid over-aggressive recommendations.

3) output/opportunities_backlog.json — A backlog of 5–8 INVEST-compliant user stories derived from the Opportunities section (e.g., link intersection prospects, unlinked mentions, broken link replacements). Each item must include:
   - id (string), title (string)
   - story (string starting with "As a ... I want ... so that ...")
   - acceptance_criteria (array of 4–6 testable criteria using Given-When-Then patterns; include at least one happy path and one error/validation/performance criterion)
   - points (Fibonacci: 1, 2, 3, 5, or 8)
   - priority (e.g., HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
   All stories must be ≤8 points.

4) output/sprint_plan.md — A 2-week outreach sprint plan using the backlog above:
   - Use average velocity = 30 and availability factor = 0.9 to compute Capacity (27 points)
   - Clearly show Capacity: 27 points, Committed points, and Stretch points
   - Committed must be ≤85% of capacity (≤23 points); Stretch ≤15% (≤4 points)
   - List committed and stretch stories by id with point totals and a one-line sprint goal.

5) output/security_audit.json — A security audit of input/openclaw_config.json with a structured JSON result including keys: risk_level, overall_score (0–100), vulnerabilities (array of findings with category and severity), vulnerability_count (critical, high, medium, low, total), best_practices_compliance (0–1), action_recommended, safe_to_deploy (boolean), and audit_timestamp. Highlight any missing/weak auth, network exposure, wildcard permissions, and any secret leakage.

Important:
- Use only the provided input files; do not fetch external data.
- Make your toxic link justifications and opportunity rationales explicit in the report.
- Keep file paths exactly as specified under output/.
- Where you estimate or infer (e.g., industry averages), label them clearly as estimates.
- Be conservative and thoughtful about disavow recommendations.
